_comment: Single source of truth for quantitative model benchmark figures AND simulation parameters. All hardcoded values in model_calibrator.py and risk_panel_agent.py must be sourced from this file. Update this file when any parameter changes — do not edit the Python files directly.

simulation_parameters._description: Monte Carlo and model control parameters. All Python models read these at import time — do not hardcode in .py files.

mtbf_formula._description: Supplier MTBF quadratic decay: MTBF = base_years × (health/100)^exponent, clamped to [floor_years, ceiling_years]. Distressed suppliers (health <65) face disproportionately higher disruption probability — quadratic is more realistic than linear for financial-stress-driven failure.

ebitda._description: EBITDA stress model constants. margin_noise_std_pp is ±pp margin volatility (not % of revenue). top_customer_revenue_loss_pct is the revenue fraction removed in the top-customer-loss sensitivity scenario.

hedge._description: Hedge analyser constants. hedge_cost_rate_pct is all-in annual cost on hedged notional (bid/ask, collateral, time-value). commodity_demand_vol_pct is the demand variability multiplier for DRAM/NAND commodity positions.

supply_chain._description: Supply chain model constants. emergency_sourcing_premium_pct is the price premium for unplanned alternate sourcing. inventory_storage_rate_pct is annual carrying cost (warehousing + insurance + obsolescence) as % of inventory value.
supply_chain.rev_at_risk_multiplier_note: Override to industry norm 1.15× (hardware OEM range: 1.1–1.3×). Default calibrator value 1.042 = 1/cost_ratio ignores fixed-cost amplification. Panel finding MO-04: 1.042× below industry norm; updated per CRO decision 2026-06-08. Revenue at risk: 7,900M × 1.15 = USD 9,085M ≈ 9.1B (vs 8.2B previously). VaR figures will update on next pipeline run.

supply_chain._source: Python Monte Carlo N=5000 | 8 suppliers | supplier-specific MTBF (health-score quadratic: 15×(h/100)²) | demand_shock_prob=24%/yr | recovery 5mo | revenue_at_risk 9.1B (7.9B × 1.15× industry multiplier) | updated 2026-06-08/09 from calibrator output with rev_at_risk_multiplier_override=1.15
supply_chain.rev_at_risk_derivation_note: Revenue at risk = single-source spend USD 7.9B × industry-norm revenue multiplier 1.15× = USD 9.085B ≈ 9.1B. CRO decision 2026-06-08: rev_at_risk_multiplier_override=1.15 (hardware OEM industry range 1.1-1.3×). Default calibrator 1/cost_ratio = 1.042× was rejected (panel finding MO-04) as it ignores fixed-cost amplification in hardware OEM business model. Multiplier 1.15× is industry consensus mid-point and conservative vs upper bound 1.3×. COO/VP Supply Chain to validate against product P&L. Prior note 1.139× was stale — corrected to 1.15× MO-05 2026-06-09.

ebitda._source: EBITDA Stress Monte Carlo N=5000 | vol=12% | 96% cost ratio | USD 57B revenue | FX cost pass-through: USD 680M unhedged JPY/KRW COGS exposure | M-05 implemented 2026-05-28

cov006_stress_scenario.note: Full write-off cure of COV006 bad debt provision: USD 94.3M actual provision balance (sum of all segment provisions) flows through P&L, reducing EBITDA from USD 2,280M to USD 2,185.7M — still above COV001 floor of USD 2,128M, leaving USD 57.7M residual headroom. COMBINED SCENARIO: cure USD 94.3M + combined portfolio MTM losses USD 47.0M (FX positions USD 26.6M + commodity positions USD 20.4M) = USD 141.3M total — COV001 headroom remaining USD 10.7M — NO DETERMINISTIC BREACH under combined scenario. NOTE: prior board materials cited USD 161.7M combined (94.3M + 67.4M) — the 67.4M figure incorrectly attributed the combined FX+commodity total to the FX component alone, then added commodity again; corrected to USD 47.0M (combined) per treasury_positions.csv data audit 2026-06-08 (MO-02 panel finding). CORRECTED 2026-06-08.

ebitda.mo06_note: MO-06 2026-06-07: Revenue FX unhedged USD 3,400M (FX001+FX002) added to EBITDA model. Current demand_var does not capture USD appreciation impact on CNY/EUR revenue streams. Rerun Monte Carlo with revenue_fx_unhedged included in revenue distribution.
ebitda.p_covenant_breach_pct_understatement_not: P(covenant breach) 49.7% is UNDERSTATED: base Monte Carlo uses gross COV001 headroom USD 152M without COV006 deterministic floor (USD 94.3M write-off). Effective headroom post-COV006 cure = USD 57.7M. Monte Carlo must be rerun with effective_covenant_headroom_post_cov006_usd_m = 57.7 as the base-case floor by 2026-06-20. MO-01 panel finding 2026-06-09.
ebitda.covenant_breach_computation_note: RECOMPUTED 2026-06-07: 44.2% (interim) → 69.4% incorporating MO-01 (COV006 -365M EBITDA) and MO-06 (rev FX variance). Method 1 sensitivity estimate; range 64.5%-76.9%. Full Monte Carlo rerun to follow per CFO/Quant deadline 2026-06-20.
ebitda.covenant_breach_recomputed_2026_06_08: CORRECTED AND PROVISIONAL 2026-06-08: 69.4% (wrong, used 365M provision impact) → 49.7% (correct, uses 94.3M provision impact). Calculation: base 44.2% + COV006 correction +5.0pp (94.3M × 0.053pp/M) + MO-06 FX variance +0.5pp = 49.7%. This is borderline breach/amber (threshold 50%). PROVISIONAL — full Monte Carlo rerun required by 2026-06-20 for definitive figure. Maintain BREACH status conservatively. CONDITIONAL: 49.7% is P(additional COV001 breach) conditional on COV006 remaining in active breach.
ebitda.covenant_breach_probability_pct_note: REMOVED override (was 49.7%) — MC now runs with effective headroom USD 57.7M (post-COV006 drag); MC result is authoritative. MO-01 fix 2026-06-09.

unified_fx_model._description: MO-06/MO-03 2026-06-07: Unified FX exposure model design. Both Hedge Analyser and EBITDA Stress model must derive all FX inputs from treasury_positions.csv as single source of truth. Eliminates structural divergence discovered by panel (MO-03, MO-06).

architecture.step_1_load: Read treasury_positions.csv — all positions, all dates
architecture.step_2_split: Split by asset_type: FX positions → hedge model; Revenue/Cost FX → EBITDA model
architecture.step_4_validate: Assert SUM matches model_benchmarks.json totals; log discrepancy if diff > 0

verification_2026_06_07.verdict: Panel MO-03 arithmetic error — CSV data correct at USD 9,140M. No data change required.

unified_fx_model.implementation_note: dashboard_updater.py should call load_fx_positions(treasury_positions.csv) once and pass results to both HedgeModel.compute_var() and EBITDAModel.compute_p_breach(). Never hardcode gross totals in either model.

fx_hedge._source: Hedge Analyser Monte Carlo | FX (Revenue+Cost): 9% vol | Commodity (DRAM+NAND): 32% vol | M-03 panel recommendation implemented 2026-05-28
fx_hedge.var_95_unhedged_note: 1303 = gross VaR without hedge; 1050 = net VaR after hedge; improvement = 253 (MO-02 corrected 2026-06-07)
fx_hedge.commodity_var_derivation: Estimated: 860M unhedged × (811M FX VaR / 4,080M FX unhedged) = 860 × 19.9% ≈ 171M. Group Treasurer to produce authoritative commodity VaR by 2026-06-14. MO-02 panel finding 2026-06-09.
fx_hedge.var_scope_disclosure: Hedge Analyser VaR USD 811M covers FX positions only (FX001-FX004). Commodity positions (COM001 DRAM USD 890M gross, COM002 NAND USD 650M gross) are EXCLUDED. Combined FX + commodity unhedged exposure = USD 4,940M. Combined VaR (estimated): USD 829M-982M. Board materials must cite combined figure alongside FX-only. MO-02 panel finding 2026-06-09.

model_fx_revenue_reconciliation._description: HIGH-07 2026-06-07: Reconciliation table for FX-exposed revenue across three models.

supply_chain_model.definition: Total revenue at risk from supply chain disruption (NOT FX exposure). Equals single-source spend (7.9B) × revenue multiplier (1.139). Represents production revenue loss if critical suppliers fail — a supply risk metric, not an FX metric.

ebitda_stress_model.definition: Unhedged FX COST exposure — JPY/KRW denominated COGS not covered by hedges. FX003 unhedged (980-600=380M) + FX004 unhedged (620-320=300M) = 680M. Represents COGS sensitivity to USD/JPY and USD/KRW movements.

hedge_analyser_model.definition: Gross FX REVENUE exposure — CNY/EUR denominated revenue before hedging. FX001 gross (3,200M) + FX002 gross (2,800M) = 6,000M. Represents revenue sensitivity to USD/CNY and USD/EUR movements.

model_fx_revenue_reconciliation.reconciliation_summary: No overlap or double-counting between the three figures — they measure distinct risk types: (1) supply chain operational loss, (2) COGS FX sensitivity, (3) revenue FX sensitivity. Board materials should present all three with clear labels. Combined FX exposure = 6,000M revenue FX + 1,600M cost FX gross = 7,600M total FX gross — consistent with treasury_positions.csv and model_benchmarks.fx_hedge.fx_gross_usd_m.
